note: Tempdoc 600 PART IX/X — the degradation-cause reason vocabulary is ONE closed authority. The producer (StatusLifecycleHandler) emits readiness reason codes from the closed LifecycleReasonCode enum; the FE CAUSE_ROWS table words them (code -> wording/remedy/severity). This register's gate (scripts/ci/check-readiness-reason-codes.mjs) enforces producer<->consumer correspondence so neither can drift: FORWARD = every enum code that is NOT exempt has a CAUSE_ROWS row (so a user can never be shown a raw `Degraded: <code>` string in the degradation banner); BACKWARD = every CAUSE_ROWS code is a real enum member OR a declared FE-derived code (no dead/typo rows). The two allow-lists below are the only curated artifact (the search-issuance gate's allowedReferences pattern).

noWordingExempt.why: NOT_CONFIGURED state on INDEX_SERVING -> verdict 'checking', never rendered by wordCauses (PART X)
NOT_READY state on INDEX_SERVING -> verdict 'checking' (reproduced live: no banner)
TELEMETRY dimension -> 'telemetry' composite, which the verdict never reads
search/RAG reason code; not emitted by the readiness producer (grep-confirmed)
search/RAG reason code; not emitted by the readiness producer

feDerived.why: Tempdoc 596 §17 — FE-only control-availability code; not emitted by the readiness producer
Tempdoc 629 #3 — FE-only: the locked-chat affordance words itself from CAUSE_ROWS; the backend never emits it as a readiness reason

Tempdoc 637 #1 — FE-only: the FE detects a dead/replaced FE→backend binding (passive staleness → verdict 'unreachable') and words it from CAUSE_ROWS; the backend cannot emit it (it is unreachable by definition)
